subject: SO override files to use apt kube dns address
commit_message:
SO override files to use apt kube dns address

All SO override files are updated with correct kube dns address for inter application communications based on review comments

Change-Id: I6e1487f3d5a7db49df3e40fb41b6b148aacfee8d
Issue-ID: SO-1022
Signed-off-by: Ramesh Parthasarathy(rp6768)<rp6768@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/386/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/386

Patch Set 1:

SO Override files are external application configuration files used by the application. I have passed on the namespace to the container as an environment variable and the application dynamically builds kube dns names based on the above variable (e.g. http://so-catalog-db-adapter.${NAMESPACE}:8082)
Please review.

Abandoned

I spoke with Brian. He guided me thru OOM approach for config files. I'll provide a new submission shortly. Thanks

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

I haven't had a chance to dive into the new SO architecture yet...how does this override.yaml file get used?  Could its purpose be served directly by values.yaml and result in a configmap?
Is there stuff that can be shared across each microservice?  if there is we usually leverage the parent chart (SO) to provide common config to the children to avoid duplication.

I did a search for simpledemo.onap.org and there are still 3 hits even after applying this patch.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

> Uploaded patch set 8.

 > (2 comments)
 > 
 > I haven't had a chance to dive into the new SO architecture
 > yet...how does this override.yaml file get used?  Could its purpose
 > be served directly by values.yaml and result in a configmap?
 > Is there stuff that can be shared across each microservice?  if
 > there is we usually leverage the parent chart (SO) to provide
 > common config to the children to avoid duplication.
 > 
 > I did a search for simpledemo.onap.org and there are still 3 hits
 > even after applying this patch.

how does this override.yaml file get used?
These override files are external application configuration files. These yaml files are mounted on the container using configmap and provided to the application during startup.

Could its purpose be served directly by values.yaml and result in a configmap?
No. These yamls have hierarchical structure that cannot be used in values file (& configmap).

Is there stuff that can be shared across each microservice?
These SO applications are independent applictions and have their own configuration.

I did a search for simpledemo.onap.org and there are still 3 hits even after applying this patch.
Good catch. I have addressed 2 of them and third one (bpmn) depends on sniro emulator and I'll address it shortly.
